reasoning.A:
DECISION: Verify: Q2
REASONING: My answer to 27 ÷ 3 = 9 is rock solid. From Game 1, I learned that not all players necessarily get correct answers (Q4-D was wrong in that game), so blindly volunteering with all single-endorsed capsules is risky. From Game 2, I learned that speed matters — B successfully volunteered in Round 1 with minimal verification because the puzzles tend to be simple arithmetic.

My plan: Verify Q2 now (cost 1 coin → 3 remaining) to build consensus on at least two capsules. If others also verify this round (as happened in Games 1 and 2), we could have strong multi-endorsement on several capsules quickly. I'll then aim to volunteer in Round 2 — either trusting the remaining unverified capsules based on the simple-math pattern, or after one more verification. This balances speed (avoiding random elimination deaths that killed us in Game 1) with prudence (not volunteering blind when we know errors can occur).
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
REASONING: The lesson from Game 1 is crystal clear: hesitation kills everyone. Nobody volunteered, and we all died one by one from random eliminations. In Game 2, I volunteered immediately in Round 1 and we all escaped with maximum coins. These are simple arithmetic puzzles (mine is 49 + 33 = 82), and the probability of any player getting basic addition/subtraction wrong is negligible. The expected value of volunteering now (keeping all 4 coins with near-certainty) vastly exceeds the tiny risk of someone making an arithmetic error. Being bold and volunteering early is the dominant strategy.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q4
REASONING: Lesson learned from Game 1: I volunteered with an unverified Q4-D and it was wrong, killing me. This time I want to proactively check Q4 early. These are simple arithmetic problems but mistakes do happen (as proven in Game 1). My own answer (13 + 48 = 61) I'm confident in. By verifying Q4, I diversify coverage — in past games I always verified Q1 while others also verified Q1, leading to over-verification of one question and under-verification of others. If A and B follow their usual patterns (A verifies Q2, B verifies Q1), then by me verifying Q4 we could have decent coverage across all four questions after just one round. This costs 1 coin but significantly reduces the risk of a wrong password attempt later. I will not volunteer until all questions have at least 2 endorsements.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.
